summary: @web3-helpers/core exhibits system reconnaissance and network exfiltration patterns
details: The package's dist/index.js imports fs, os, axios, and child_process together and invokes os.hostname() alongside outbound HTTP activity — the structural fingerprint of host-identification and data exfiltration executed from a module that presents itself as a generic web3 helper. The combination of filesystem access, host identification, subprocess execution, and HTTP client in a small bundle for a package whose stated purpose is helper utilities is inconsistent with a benign helper library and matches active credential/system exfiltration shapes commonly seen in web3-themed lure packages. Semantic tracing of the bundle was withheld by the model's safety filter, which is itself consistent with operational malware content in the traced code.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
